LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS OSCAR PEREZ, CHRISTINA PEREZ AND OSCAR PEREZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM MAY-16-2007 TO LAW AND MOTION AT MAY-21-2007, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS OSCAR PEREZ, CHRISTINA PEREZ AND OSCAR PEREZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: 1. DEMURRER TO FIRST AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED, WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, FOR UNCERTAINTY. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF MAY BE ALLEGING SEPARATE WRONGS BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINTS ABOUT THE PROPERTY AND DEFENDANT'S STEPS TO REMOVE THE PROPERTY UNDER THE ELLIS ACT. DEMURRERS FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION ARE OVERRULED. THE FILING OF AN ELLIS ACT NOTICE, IN AND OF ITSELF, IS NOT NECESSARILY A PART OF THE LITIGATION PROCESS BECAUSE IT IS MEANT TO BE A SELF-EXECUTING STATUTE. WHETHER A NOTICE MAY BE A BASIS FOR RETALIATION IS A QUESTION OF FACT UNDER DROUET VERSUS SUPERIOR COURT (2003) 31 CAL. 4TH 583. 2. DEMURRERS TO SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JOANN PRIOR, CSR 	129

MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Raquel Fox, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Mireya Garcia, Ofilio Blanco and Elba Salazar Garcia
Michel Hall, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Oscar Perez

After hearing, Plaintiff's motion to compel attendance at deposition or for evidentiary sanctions is granted. Depostions to be completed by May 19, 2008. If not, defendants prohibited form testifying. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS OSCAR PEREZ, CHRISTINA PEREZ AND OSCAR PEREZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: RAQUEL FOX, ESQUIRE (TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, 415.771.9850) APPEARING FOR PLAILNTIFF. MICHAEL C. HALL, ESQUIRE (415.512.9865) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: 1. DEMURRER TO FIRST AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED, WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, FOR UNCERTAINTY. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF MAY BE ALLEGING SEPARATE WRONGS BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINTS ABOUT THE PROPERTY AND DEFENDANT'S STEPS TO REMOVE THE PROPERTY UNDER THE ELLIS ACT. DEMURRERS FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION ARE OVERRULED. THE FILING OF AN ELLIS ACT NOTICE, IN AND OF ITSELF, IS NOT NECESSARILY A PART OF THE LITIGATION PROCESS BECAUSE IT IS MEANT TO BE A SELF-EXECUTING STATUTE. WHETHER A NOTICE MAY BE A BASIS FOR RETALIATION IS A QUESTION OF FACT UNDER DROUET VERSUS SUPERIOR COURT (2003) 31 CAL. 4TH 583. 2. DEMURRERS TO SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/AP) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JOANN PRIOR, CSR 129
